title: Scattering and Bound States of Two Heteronuclear Ultracold Atoms in a Quasi-Two-Dimensional Confinement
authors: B. Wang, F. Yang, P. Zhang
abstract: We solve the two-body problem of ultracold heteronuclear atoms in a quasi-two-dimensional (quasi-2D) geometry. The quasi-2D confinement is realized by a harmonic trap along the longitudinal ($z$-) direction, with different trap frequencies for the two atoms, as in many current experiments on ultracold heteronuclear gases. As a consequence, the longitudinal center-of-mass (CoM) motion is coupled to the relative motion, which significantly complicates the two-body problem. We solve this problem exactly and derive the 2D scattering length $a_{\rm 2D}$, the 2D effective range parameter $R_{\rm 2D}$, and the bound-state energies, as functions of the $s$-wave scattering length and effective range of the two atoms in free three-dimensional (3D) space. We show that multiple 2D scattering resonances can be induced by the coupling between the longitudinal CoM and relative motion. Around these resonances, $a_{\rm 2D}$ varies rapidly with the 3D scattering parameters, while $R_{\rm 2D}$ is strongly enhanced. Since the effective pairwise interaction in quasi-2D ultracold gases is determined by i.e., the two-body scattering amplitudes and bound-state energies, our results can be used for manipulating the effective 2D interatomic interaction in quasi-2D ultracold heteronuclear gases by tuning the confinement frequencies and the 3D scattering parameters.
summary: This paper provides an exact theoretical solution to the two-body problem for heteronuclear ultracold atoms in a quasi-2D trap. By analyzing the coupling between the center-of-mass and relative motion, the authors derive expressions for the effective 2D scattering parameters. The results demonstrate that these parameters can be rapidly tuned by adjusting the trap frequencies and the underlying 3D scattering interactions, offering a powerful tool for manipulating interatomic forces.
main_problem: To solve the two-body scattering problem for heteronuclear ultracold atoms confined in a quasi-two-dimensional geometry.
main_result: The study derives the 2D scattering length ($a_{2D}$) and effective range ($R_{2D}$), showing that the coupling between longitudinal center-of-mass and relative motion can induce multiple 2D scattering resonances.
method: The problem is solved exactly by treating it as a multichannel system, involving the separation of the center-of-mass motion and solving coupled integral equations for the scattering and bound states.
model_or_system:
The system consists of two heteronuclear ultracold atoms trapped in a quasi-2D harmonic potential, where the longitudinal confinement frequencies for the two atoms differ ($\omega_1 
eq \omega_2$). The interaction is modeled by a zero-range pseudopotential characterized by 3D scattering parameters.
key_observables: 2D scattering length ($a_{2D}$), 2D effective range ($R_{2D}$), and bound-state energies ($E_b$).
important_parameters: Longitudinal confinement frequencies ($\omega_1, \omega_2$), 3D scattering length ($a_{3D}$), and 3D effective range ($R_{3D}$).
main_assumptions: The analysis is restricted to the low-energy regime, and the derivation relies on short-range expansions and the use of Hadamard finite part integrals to regularize divergences.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the schematic confinement setup, the dependence of $a_{2D}$ and $R_{2D}$ on $a_{3D}$ for different frequency ratios ($\omega_1 > \omega_2$ vs $\omega_1 < \omega_2$), and the multichannel structure of the Hamiltonian.
paper_structure: The paper systematically derives the scattering and bound state properties by first setting up the coupled Hamiltonian, then solving the resulting Lippmann-Schwinger type integral equations for the scattering state, and finally deriving the homogeneous integral equation for the bound state energy.
why_interesting: This work provides a detailed theoretical framework for controlling effective interactions in highly confined quantum systems, which is crucial for designing quantum simulators and realizing novel quantum phenomena.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a): Schematic diagram of two heteronuclear ultra- cold atoms in a quasi-2D confinement, which is realized via a strong confinement along the longitudinal (z-) direction. (b): Schematic diagram of the harmonic longitudinal confinement potentials V (1) and V (2), experienced by atoms 1 and 2, re- spectively.

FIG. 2. The 2D scattering length a2D and effective range parameter R2D for a 6Li-53Cr system (m1 : m2 = 6 : 53), in the case ω1 > ω2. In panels (a–d) and (e–h) show the results for ω1 : ω2 = 6 : 1 and ω1 : ω2 = 2 : 1, respec- tively. For both frequency ratios, the results are presented as functions of ℓz/a3D, with R3D = 0 in panels (a,c,e,g) and R3D = 6ℓz in panels (b,d,f,h). Here ℓz = p

FIG. 3. The 2D scattering length a2D and effective range pa- rameter R2D for a 6Li-53Cr system (m1 : m2 = 6 : 53), in the case ω1 < ω2. In panels (a–d) and (e–h) show the results for ω1 : ω2 = 1 : 2 and ω1 : ω2 = 1 : 6, respectively. For both fre- quency ratios, the results are presented as functions of ℓz/a3D, with R3D = 0 in panels (a,c,e,g) and R3D = 6ℓz in panels (b,d,f,h). Here ℓz = p

FIG. 4. Schematic illustration of the multichannel structure of the system. For clarity, only the lowest three CoM chan- nels are shown. The solid red lines represent the threshold energies of the channels, the red dashed lines denote scatter- ing states, and the blue dots indicate the bound states in the corresponding channels. The orange lines represent the inter- channel couplings induced by the term gzZ. For details, see Sec. III A.

FIG. 5. Bound-state energies Eb of a 6Li-53Cr system with m1 : m2 = 6 : 53 and ω1 : ω2 = 6 : 1. Panels (a) and (b) show the results for R3D = 0 and R3D = 6ℓz, respectively. Here Ethre ≡(ω1 +ω2)/2 is the scattering threshold, corresponding to the longitudinal zero-point energy. The solid and dashed lines represent the bound states in the P = +1 and P = −1 subspaces, respectively.
